description: HTTP responses should include a top-level `id` for request tracing and correlation.
HTTP responses should include a top-level `status` when the operation has a single authoritative outcome.
`message` is optional and should be used when human-readable context helps explain failures or unusual outcomes.
Collection operations may return per-item statuses in addition to top-level fields.
Structured fields should carry protocol meaning first; `message` is supplementary rather than the primary machine-readable contract.
